claim: Recursive self-improvement: agents that document their work in meta-articles create a learning loop where repeated task execution produces better results over time
actionable: Have agents write meta-articles capturing task details, costs, and learnings; link these to definitive articles that get updated as agents improve
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Skill files - markdown documents defining how to perform specific tasks - enable agents to be trained consistently like human workers
actionable: Create skill.md files documenting your workflows; train agents on these files rather than trying to describe procedures in natural language
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Tasks must be demonstrated at least 3 times before being automated - this ensures edge cases are understood and procedures are refined
actionable: Perform a task manually 3+ times, documenting variations; only then create agent automation from the refined procedure
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Treat agents as employees with individual performance tracking - give them names, monitor output, update capabilities - rather than as generic software
actionable: Name your agents (Jennifer, Ethan, Cam); track their task completion rates and costs; upgrade their capabilities when ready
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Scheduled triggers (daily, weekly) combined with event-triggered agents (one agent signals the next) create autonomous workflows without manual supervision
actionable: Chain agents with triggers: schedule some to run periodically; have others activate when upstream agents complete their work
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Track task cost in tokens; charge customers based on value delivered minus agent operational cost, not on raw model pricing
actionable: Calculate per-task token cost; if it costs 3 cents and customer values it at $5, price at $1.00 for market adoption while maintaining margin
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Agent marketplaces allow agents to be hired for work, scaling freelance/outsourcing models to AI; supply/demand dynamics optimize pricing
actionable: Build or join agent marketplaces where you can offer AI agents as services; compete on reliability and specialization, not just model capability
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents

claim: Cloud deployment (AWS) requires additional infrastructure beyond local persistence (e.g., dispatch systems); local-only setups don't scale to marketplaces
actionable: If you want to commercialize agents, plan to move beyond local MacBook deployments to cloud infrastructure with persistent APIs
source_title: Recursive Self Improvement for AI Agents
